titleStmt.title: Letter from John F. Smyth to Robert Chalmers, 25 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John F. Smyth

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from a Monaghan based solicitor John F. Smyth to the under-secretary Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). After the Easter Rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. Smyth is writhing on behalf of Miss Kate Rooney, and is asking for a response for a previous letter which included a petition from Miss Rooney for a Temporary License for her premises in Castleblayney, Monaghan. Smyth is asking for a quick reply as the answer is needed before the end of the month.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8909/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Castleblayney,
Co. Monaghan. 25th May, 1916.
Re James Rooney Dead Sir, I shall feel obliged to you letting
me know the result of Miss Rooney's
Petition sent you on the 13th inst. The
matter is rather urgent and I would be
glad to hear from you before the end of
the week. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John F. Smyth The Under Secretary,
The Castle,
Dublin.
